data.title: Microservices Framework
data.description: YouTube's architecture includes a collection of microservices that handle separate functions independently.
data.technical_spec: Built using Google infrastructure components like Borg (container orchestration), Bigtable (data storage), and Colossus (file storage).

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A global network of servers that caches video content to deliver it efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes a combination of Google's own CDN and partnerships with external CDNs for redundancy and speed.

data.title: Scalable Video Processing
data.description: System for encoding and processing videos to different formats and resolutions.
data.technical_spec: Leverages Google's internal tools like TensorFlow for processing and optimizing video delivery.

data.title: NoSQL Database Systems
data.description: Datastore for managing metadata and user information.
data.technical_spec: Primarily uses Google's Bigtable and Spanner for distributed data management.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: A method to distribute incoming traffic efficiently to servers hosting YouTube services.
data.technical_spec: Integrated with Google's global load balancing infrastructure.
